metadata_update.thinking_parameter_used: top-level body['think'] = false; HOWEVER model still emits thinking-style text (e.g. 'Let me think...') and </think> token in the response field even with think:false. Suppressing thinking completely is not achievable with this Ollama version (0.32.1) on this model.
metadata_update.recommendation: GO-WITH-CAVEATS (as a faster-than-gemma4:26b local model for power users) / NO-GO (as default primary replacement for gemma4:26b on OC2A 48GB)
metadata_update.recommendation_short: qwen3:30b is the fastest local model tested on OC2A (71.2 t/s short vs 57.4 for gemma4:26b) but requires forced num_ctx<=4096 (default 262144 OOMs the M4 Pro 48GB) and leaks thinking-style text into responses even with think:false. Keep gemma4:26b as primary local model. qwen3:30b can stay pulled (18 GB on disk) for manual experimentation but should not be the default.

key_metrics.architecture: qwen3moe (30.5B total params, 3.3B active per token)
key_metrics.pull_errors: none (initial long-exec was SIGKILLed; resume from 90% completed cleanly)
key_metrics.thinking_bug_disabled_via_top_level_thin: partial — model still emits reasoning preambles in response field

comparison_to_tkt1014.qwen3_30b_verdict: GO-WITH-CAVEATS (faster, but operational gymnastics required) / NO-GO (as drop-in replacement)

comparison_to_tkt1021.winner_for_qwen3_tier: qwen3:30b (MoE 3.3B active is much faster than qwen3:32b dense)
comparison_to_tkt1021.qwen3_32b_status: REMOVED FROM DISK per Ken (TKT-1021 follow-up)
comparison_to_tkt1021.qwen3_30b_status: Pulled; can stay on OC2A (18 GB) or be removed
